library.notes: Residual verdicts for 8 heuristic C5↔ISO pairs not covered by the official 205-pair crosswalk. The official amtlich table already covers the strong pairs; these are the boundary / overlap / contested cases.

verdicts.note: OPS-01 is fully covered by the official crosswalk (OIS-02 → A.5.1). No duplicate heuristic mapping warranted.
OIS-05 is mapped to A.5.5 in the official table, not A.5.7. The heuristic pair conflates Threat Intelligence with Contact with Authorities. Refuted.
IDM-01 (Identity Management) maps to A.5.16 User Identity Management in the official table. A.8.1 (User Endpoint Devices) is not the correct ISO control.
SSO-04 (Security of Network Services) maps to A.8.20/A.8.21 in the official table. A.5.23 (Information security for use of cloud services) is a consumer-side control, not a provider-side mapping.

IDM-09 (Privileged Access Management) has strong overlap with A.8.5 (Secure Authentication). The official crosswalk maps IDM-09 to A.5.18 (Access Rights). Needs expert reconciliation: both controls may apply.

OPS-15 (Protection Against Malware) maps to A.8.7 in the official crosswalk with SN=0, confirming the pair. However the heuristic mapping omits the additional A.8.8 (Management of technical vulnerabilities) link present in the official table. Needs completeness review.
OPS-10 (Logging and Monitoring) officially maps to A.8.15 and A.8.16. The heuristic pair covers only A.8.16 (Monitoring Activities). Needs review to add A.8.15 (Logging) as co-mapping.

IDM-02 (User Registration and De-registration) officially maps to A.5.16 (User Identity Management) and A.5.18 (Access Rights). A.5.15 (Access Control) is a broader policy control. Needs review: A.5.15 may be an acceptable supplementary mapping.
